name: semantic_duplicate_redescribe
description: 10-turn behavioral regression test for the CB semantic-variant suppression rule + ObjectHelper soft-match guard. Starts in Ken Williams' office (Sierra On-Line, 1987). Discovery turns T1-T5 populate the object registry. Re-description turns T6, T8, T10 (narrow re-observation of specific known objects) assert promoted === 0 -- proving CB recognized already-known objects and did not create new records. T7 (broad look-around) intentionally has no suppression assertion: one promotion is a legitimate new discovery (binder with flowcharts, never previously extracted as a discrete object); one is a timing artifact (photo frame origin-blocked on T6 before any ObjectRecord existed, correctly promoted as floor object on T7 -- not a suppression failure). World geometry is seed-deterministic; narration and extraction output vary across runs -- assertions use behavioral invariants only.
world_prompt: My name is Ken williams. My wife's name is roberta. She's not home right now. But I am, working at my desk on the finishing touches of what is to soon become our most popular game ever. I open the game and stare at the title screen which says....

turns.action: look over the papers on the desk
glance at the photo on the desk
look at the stack of design documents
look at the monitor screen carefully
